source: Elevated RDW can indicate increased red blood cell production variability, which can be seen in psoriasis.
Anemia (low Hgb) is common in psoriasis due to chronic inflammation and potential iron deficiency.
Low Hct is associated with anemia, a frequent finding in psoriasis.
Elevated WBC count can indicate inflammation, which is a hallmark of psoriasis.
Elevated platelet count (thrombocytosis) can be seen in psoriasis due to inflammation.
Low MCV (microcytosis) can be associated with iron deficiency anemia, a potential complication of psoriasis.
Low MCH (mean corpuscular hemoglobin) can be seen in iron deficiency anemia, a potential complication of psoriasis.
Low MCHc (mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration) can be seen in iron deficiency anemia, a potential complication of psoriasis.
Low RBC count is associated with anemia, a frequent finding in psoriasis.
